2 min read
Outside of our RBS, we don’t ‘suppress’ subscribers from receiving
our VNLs when they self-select to receive an SOS/SSP/PLS, but we
appreciate there are use cases where this could be useful.
Let’s look at how to make this happen.
As described in the previous section, there are a few ways to do
this. We think using a custom �eld is the best approach.
As with an entry-point RBS, all we need to do is change the value
of our vnl_status custom �eld to Muted (or whatever value
you’ve chosen to use to signify that a subscriber should be
suppress from receiving a VNL), like this:
How To “Mute” VNLs (when in
SOSs/SSPs/PLSs)
www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

In this example, we set the vnl_status 	custom �eld to Muted
just before adding the subscriber to the SOS -- TDB Showcase
Series sequence. Once the series is �nished, the vnl_status
custom �eld is changed back to Subscribed .
In this scenario, when a subscriber is receiving the ‘SOS — TDB
Showcase Series,’ they will be omitted from all VNL broadcasts.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

If you want to focus your audience’s attention, this same logic can
be applied to any series.
If you have a question about this, please leave comments below.
Please ask questions and leave comments below.
—André & Shawn
www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 5 --

www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 5 --
